issues: Fundamentals report has a miner revenue $0 data anomaly — unreliable data passed through without automated flagging
Settlement volume -76% and NVT ~684 cited by bear but not independently verified against on-chain source
Aggressive risk analyst pushed for a contrarian long but didn't specify a concrete invalidation level — more narrative than plan
Reports are truncated in the chain — analysts may be hitting token limits, losing tail-end analysis
improvements: Add automated data quality checks before reports reach debate stage — flag $0 revenue, missing fields, stale prices
Require the aggressive analyst to submit a structured entry/stop/target when advocating action, not just qualitative arguments
Track F&G streaks and funding rate persistence as quantitative features rather than relying on analysts to remember and cite them ad hoc
Log full reports or confirm completeness — multiple reports appear cut off mid-sentence
summary: Strong decision day. The system correctly identified a bearish structure weakened by momentum exhaustion and extreme fear, then disciplined itself into cash rather than forcing a low-conviction short into max event risk and adverse funding. The debate was genuinely adversarial — the aggressive analyst made a real case for a contrarian long that was properly weighed and rejected. Main gap is data hygiene: the fundamentals report passed through known-bad data without automated flagging.
